Canada: Co-hosts flying; first side to clinch a Round of 16 spot after a dramatic win over South Africa.
Morocco: Atlas Lions dumped the Netherlands out on penalties to reach a third-ever Round of 16.
Paraguay: Giant-killers stunned Germany on penalties for their first Round of 16 since 2010.
France: Mbappé leads the Golden Boot race as the favourites eye a deep knockout run.
Brazil: Neymar back from a calf injury off the bench; Vinícius Júnior set for a Haaland showdown.
Norway: Haaland's brace ended a near-30-year World Cup goal drought and he's on fire.
Mexico: Co-hosts buzzing after a first knockout win since 1986, roared on by a home crowd.
England: Kane's brace sparked a comeback, but Reece James is out and Declan Rice nursing back pain.
Portugal: Ronaldo broke his World Cup knockout duck from the spot ahead of an all-Iberian clash.
Spain: Double injury blow as wingers Nico Williams and Yeremy Pino limped out against Uruguay.
USA: Co-hosts into the last 16 and dreaming big in front of raucous home support.
Belgium: Golden generation grinding on after seeing off Senegal to reach the knockouts.
Argentina: Defending champions survived a Cabo Verde scare and now hunt their ruthless edge.
Egypt: Egypt won its first-ever knockout tie on penalties, but Salah's knee is a fitness worry.
Switzerland: Dark horses aiming to spring an upset on a fancied Colombia side.
Colombia: Colombia edged Ghana 1–0 for a third last-16 spot in four World Cups.
